sources.writer_profile: Marti Buckley is an American food writer and journalist who has lived in San Sebastián since 2010. She is the author of Basque Country: A Culinary Journey Through a Food Lover's Paradise (Ten Speed Press, 2018) and a co-founder of Culinary Backstreets. Her Bilbao writing draws on over a decade of regular visits and focuses on the bars and restaurants that define everyday eating for locals rather than tourists.
sources.article_title: The Essentials: Where to Eat in Bilbao, Spain

sources.writer_profile: Mairenis Gómez is a Bilbao-based writer who covers local gastronomy and city culture for bilbaohiria.com. Her pintxos coverage centres on the bars favoured by bilbaínos rather than tourists, with particular attention to Plaza Nueva and the streets of Casco Viejo where the best txikiteo routes take shape.
sources.article_title: De pintxos por Bilbao: 10 bares imprescindibles en 2024

sources.writer_profile: Moani Hood is the sole author of Appetites Abroad, an independent food and travel blog focused on culinary experiences across Europe and Latin America. Her Bilbao coverage, published August 2025, draws on a first-hand visit and combines classic pintxos bar recommendations with off-the-beaten-path spots.
sources.article_title: Savoring Bilbao: A Foodie's Guide on Where to Eat in Bilbao, Spain

sources.writer_profile: Irenie Forshaw is a food and travel writer who contributes to The Week magazine, covering destination restaurant guides across Spain and Europe. Her Bilbao guide spans the full spectrum from historic pintxos bars to Michelin-starred dining rooms and was published in June 2024.

Igor Cubillo is a veteran Spanish food journalist and critic based in the Basque Country. He is the founder and director of Lo que Coma Don Manuel (loquecomadonmanuel.com), one of Spain's most respected independent food criticism platforms, and has written about gastronomy for national publications for over two decades. His annual year-end list of best Bilbao restaurants is drawn from visits throughout the year and reflects an exceptionally deep familiarity with the city's dining scene.

restaurants.name: Taberna Basaras
restaurants.quote: Basaras was founded in 1940, and its tiny interior is packed to the brim with wine bottles; a bar that manages to stick around for over eight decades, watching a faithful flow of regulars ebb in and out, achieves heroic status.
restaurants.article_title: The Essentials: Where to Eat in Bilbao, Spain

restaurants.name: Bodega Joserra
restaurants.quote: Opened in 1924, the fourth generation is behind the bar of this locally beloved spot, serving simple, good wine with bite-size baguette sandwiches; a pile of bonito con divisa sits out before service.
restaurants.article_title: The Essentials: Where to Eat in Bilbao, Spain

restaurants.name: Café Iruña
restaurants.quote: Opened in 1903, its tables have served as the setting for tertulias — social and literary gatherings — among prestigious writers and poets; order the pintxo moruno, a lamb kebab heady with spices.
restaurants.article_title: The Essentials: Where to Eat in Bilbao, Spain

restaurants.name: Gure Toki
dishes_mentioned: Idiazabal cheese soup with quail egg and porcini
restaurants.quote: Few bars have won more awards than this spot, so come hungry and ready to be surprised — Pedro Siles and his wife Santiaga opened this mecca of the modern pintxo in the 1980s.
restaurants.article_title: The Essentials: Where to Eat in Bilbao, Spain

restaurants.name: Asador Indusi
restaurants.quote: Bilbao's traditional asadores (grill houses) remain the heart of its culinary identity; here you'll find grilled turbot, hake kokotxas in salsa verde, and tender oxtail stew alongside croquetas caseras and txangurro gratinado.
restaurants.article_title: The Essentials: Where to Eat in Bilbao, Spain

restaurants.name: Txakoli Simón
restaurants.quote: Sip from a cold bottle of the local white wine and await your sizzling txuleta steak, which arrives smoking over charcoal on a personal-sized grill — Óscar García Baldeón achieved the democratisation of the txuleta at an accessible price.
restaurants.article_title: The Essentials: Where to Eat in Bilbao, Spain

restaurants.name: Rio-Oja
restaurants.quote: Industrial aluminum pots filled with various shades of brown line the bar, from snails in a slightly spicy tomato creation to meatballs — you'll find more variations on the theme than you ever thought possible.
restaurants.article_title: The Essentials: Where to Eat in Bilbao, Spain

restaurants.name: Bar El Eme
restaurants.quote: Bar Eme is famous for its legendary triángulos — sandwiches that are gloriously sloppy — served with a mysterious salsa rossini, a spicy red sauce that keeps locals coming back; the bar has maintained its artisan spirit through generations.
restaurants.article_title: The Essentials: Where to Eat in Bilbao, Spain

restaurants.name: Irrintzi
dishes_mentioned: octopus in red wine with vinaigrette
restaurants.quote: Pintxos like octopus cooked in red wine until tender and drizzled with acidic vinaigrette, and fragrant falafel with shiitake mushroom make this a rare vegetarian-friendly stop — and the duck confit with a kick of peanut is famous.
restaurants.article_title: The Essentials: Where to Eat in Bilbao, Spain

restaurants.name: Bikandi Etxea
restaurants.quote: Bikandi Etxea specialises in cazuelas — the local term for stews and amply sauced dishes — including a salt cod al pil-pil: tender flaky cod draped in silky yellow sauce, eaten at tables covered in homey blue-and-white-checked tablecloths.
restaurants.article_title: The Essentials: Where to Eat in Bilbao, Spain

restaurants.name: Café Bar Bilbao
dishes_mentioned: tortilla rellena de jamón y queso
restaurants.quote: Ubicado en la Plaza Nueva, este bar mantiene un ambiente local a pesar de su ubicación turística. Destacan los pintxos de bacalao al pil pil, los canutillos de queso idiazábal, la chistorra y la morcilla con manzana.
restaurants.article_title: De pintxos por Bilbao: 10 bares imprescindibles en 2024

restaurants.name: Bar Charly
dishes_mentioned: pintxo de bonito con cebolla encurtida
pintxo de jamón, queso manchego y alioli
restaurants.signature_dish: pintxo de jamón, queso manchego y alioli
restaurants.quote: También en la Plaza Nueva, el bar Charly atrae con su barra llena de pintxos dispuestos en varias alturas. Recomendamos la croqueta de bacalao, el pintxo de bonito con cebolla encurtida y el de pulpo.
restaurants.article_title: De pintxos por Bilbao: 10 bares imprescindibles en 2024

restaurants.name: Bacaicoa
restaurants.quote: Este bar no es especialmente turístico y tiene una clientela fiel. Sus champiñones a la gloria, las setas al purgatorio y la croqueta de boletus son destacables, pero el chorizo al infierno es la estrella.
restaurants.article_title: De pintxos por Bilbao: 10 bares imprescindibles en 2024

restaurants.name: Baste
dishes_mentioned: mejillón con relleno y bechamel rebozado y frito
restaurants.quote: Cerca de la Plaza Miguel de Unamuno, Baste tiene una clientela de toda la vida. Los mejillones rellenos son su pintxo estrella: un buen mejillón con un relleno exquisito y una capa de bechamel, rebozado y frito a la perfección.
restaurants.article_title: De pintxos por Bilbao: 10 bares imprescindibles en 2024

restaurants.name: Berton
restaurants.quote: El Berton, amplio y con zona de comedor, destaca por su barra de pintxos. El solomillo es el rey, con pintxos como las txapelas y los medallones con foie o huevo de codorniz. Las rabas también son altamente recomendadas.
restaurants.article_title: De pintxos por Bilbao: 10 bares imprescindibles en 2024

restaurants.name: Fermín
dishes_mentioned: bocatita de jamón ibérico con roquefort
restaurants.quote: Fermín apuesta más por la calidad que por la cantidad. Sus gildas son famosas, pero también destacan el bacalao al pil pil y el bocatita de jamón ibérico con roquefort.
restaurants.article_title: De pintxos por Bilbao: 10 bares imprescindibles en 2024

restaurants.name: Motrikes
dishes_mentioned: champiñones a la plancha sobre pan
restaurants.quote: En la calle Somera, el ambiente local es espectacular, especialmente los fines de semana. Los champiñones a la plancha sobre una rebanada de pan son simples pero deliciosos.
restaurants.article_title: De pintxos por Bilbao: 10 bares imprescindibles en 2024

restaurants.name: Con B de Bilbao
restaurants.quote: Este bar tiene una de las barras de pintxos más completas de Bilbao. Cada pintxo es una obra de arte, con una gran elaboración. Los quesos, las carnes, los huevos, los pescados y las verduras se destacan en su oferta.
restaurants.article_title: De pintxos por Bilbao: 10 bares imprescindibles en 2024

restaurants.name: Víctor Montes
restaurants.quote: Este histórico bar en el casco viejo tiene una de las barras de pintxos más espectaculares de Bilbao. El pintxo de txangurro es un imprescindible, y su oferta de mariscos es especialmente destacable. Desde 1849, ha evolucionado pero mantiene su encanto y calidad.
restaurants.article_title: De pintxos por Bilbao: 10 bares imprescindibles en 2024

restaurants.name: Taska Beltz
restaurants.quote: This wine bar located in the Casco Viejo neighborhood serves up small, elevated plates of traditional Spanish foods in addition to having an excellent wine selection — txipirones encebollados and talos are among the standouts.
restaurants.article_title: Savoring Bilbao: A Foodie's Guide on Where to Eat in Bilbao, Spain

restaurants.name: Lucky Baster
restaurants.quote: Lucky Baster drew me in with their fun vibes and oxtail bikini on the menu — a pressed sandwich filled with rich oxtail that is one of the most memorable bites in Bilbao.
restaurants.article_title: Savoring Bilbao: A Foodie's Guide on Where to Eat in Bilbao, Spain

restaurants.name: La Viña del Ensanche
dishes_mentioned: toasted baguette with Iberian ham and olive oil
restaurants.signature_dish: toasted baguette with Iberian ham and olive oil
restaurants.quote: Head to this classic pintxos bar for a great taste of Bilbao. The star snack is toasted baguette topped with Iberian ham and olive oil, or you can opt for the seven-dish tasting menu. Wash everything down with draught beer or the superbly priced wines.
restaurants.article_title: A foodie guide to Bilbao

restaurants.name: El Globo
restaurants.quote: It takes just one bite to see why El Globo is one of the most popular bars in Bilbao. The gleaming counter heaves under the weight of gorgeous miniature culinary creations — this is pintxo heaven, and they are known for their creamy gratin pintxos served on little pieces of bread.
restaurants.article_title: A foodie guide to Bilbao

restaurants.name: Casa Rufo
restaurants.quote: This charming restaurant began life as a grocery store in 1955. Now it's a small rustic eatery helmed by head chef José Luís Pérez Landeta, specialising in traditional Basque dishes and grilled meats — the cosy atmosphere and friendly service make you feel immediately at home.
restaurants.article_title: A foodie guide to Bilbao

restaurants.name: Kate Zaharra
restaurants.quote: Set within a farmhouse in the hills of Artxanda, Kate Zaharra is a dining experience you'll remember for years to come — the views are unparalleled and the txuleta is among the best in the city.
restaurants.article_title: A foodie guide to Bilbao

restaurants.name: Zarate
restaurants.quote: The guiding principles of this Michelin-starred restaurant are based around chef Sergio Ortiz de Zarate's exceptional knowledge of fish — the menu is built around Cantabria's finest seafood, sourced daily from the ports of Lekeitio and Ondarroa.
restaurants.article_title: A foodie guide to Bilbao

restaurants.name: Nerua Guggenheim Bilbao
restaurants.quote: Described as a cool white shrine to cutting-edge Spanish gastronomy, Nerua sits inside the Guggenheim. Chef Josean Alija's meticulous dishes draw on traditional Basque flavours and change with the seasons — the restaurant scooped a Michelin star within six months of opening.
restaurants.article_title: A foodie guide to Bilbao

restaurants.name: Amaren
restaurants.quote: Ranked 31st among the world's best steakhouses on the World's 101 Best Steak Restaurants list, Amaren presents txuletas from various cattle breeds aged to different specifications — its standout interior design is as carefully considered as the beef itself.
restaurants.article_title: Mis mejores restaurantes de Bilbao

restaurants.name: El Arandia de Julen
restaurants.quote: Punto de peregrinación para los muchos amantes de la Santísima Trinidad de la cocina vasca de cuchara: alubias rojas con sacramentos, sopa de pescado y porrusalda — tres platos que encarnan la tradición bilbaína en su estado más puro.
restaurants.article_title: Mis mejores restaurantes de Bilbao

restaurants.name: La Despensa del Etxanobe
restaurants.quote: Fernando Canales ha construido un espacio en el que se come realmente bien sin necesidad de efectismos — la merluza, en sus distintas versiones (frita, a la ondarresa, rellena), es la gran protagonista.
restaurants.article_title: Mis mejores restaurantes de Bilbao

restaurants.name: Los Fueros
restaurants.quote: El decano de los restaurantes de Bilbao, en el Casco Viejo desde 1878, recuperado con gran acierto por el chef Paul Ibarra — se mantienen las gambas que dieron fama al antiguo Bar Los Fueros y el bacalao buñuelo que convence a todo el mundo.
restaurants.article_title: Mis mejores restaurantes de Bilbao

restaurants.name: Kuma
restaurants.quote: Dani Lomana dirige una propuesta japonesa donde tartares, sashimis y nigiris se rematan lo mismo con tocineta que con producto tan rutilante como el wagyu A5 — comer en la barra es la mejor forma de disfrutar del espectáculo.
restaurants.article_title: Mis mejores restaurantes de Bilbao

restaurants.name: Lasai
restaurants.quote: David García Pereda cocina con una visión contemporánea de la gastronomía vasca — lubina, bacalao, rodaballo, merluza, ciervo, pichón; los productos se suceden en platos de gran limpieza, y el solomillo Wellington resulta memorable.
restaurants.article_title: Mis mejores restaurantes de Bilbao

restaurants.name: Mina
restaurants.quote: Álvaro Garrido propone una cocina moderna y atrevida sin etiquetas, relatos ni ataduras, que busca el compromiso con el producto local de temporada — veinticinco comensales por servicio en un espacio mínimo que concentra toda la atención en el plato.
restaurants.article_title: Mis mejores restaurantes de Bilbao

restaurants.name: Ola Martín Berasategui
restaurants.signature_dish: milhojas caramelizado de anguila ahumada con foie gras
restaurants.quote: Raúl Cabrera dirige este restaurante gastronómico del Casco Viejo bajo la arquitectura culinaria de Martín Berasategui — el milhojas caramelizado de anguila ahumada, foie gras, cebolleta y manzana verde sigue siendo un plato de referencia.
restaurants.article_title: Mis mejores restaurantes de Bilbao

restaurants.name: Porrue
restaurants.quote: Unai Campo consigue que el mejor producto, con nombre y apellido, se pose sobre la parrilla — almejas a la carbonara, txuleta de buey, setas de temporada con huevo ecológico de Orduña y navajas a la brasa componen una carta de precisión.
restaurants.article_title: Mis mejores restaurantes de Bilbao

restaurants.name: Serantes I
dishes_mentioned: Gulf of Biscay seafood and fish
restaurants.quote: El clasicismo manda en Serantes I, con su amplio horario de cocina y una carta donde el pescado y el marisco del Cantábrico son protagonistas indiscutibles — rabas, piquillos y mariscos de primera para comer sin prisas.
restaurants.article_title: Mis mejores restaurantes de Bilbao

restaurants.name: Waman
restaurants.quote: Gabriel Huaman aborda ceviches, tiraditos, causas y ají de gallina con una perspectiva de alta cocina y ánimo de fusión con la riqueza culinaria del País Vasco — fermented vegetables replace fumet in his signature ceviche.
restaurants.article_title: Mis mejores restaurantes de Bilbao

restaurants.name: Yandiola
dishes_mentioned: arroz meloso de crustáceos y alma de mar
restaurants.quote: Ricardo Pérez y Borja Etxebarria dirigen este luminoso comedor en La Alhóndiga — el steak tartar de solomillo y el arroz meloso de crustáceos y alma de mar son sus especialidades más aplaudidas.
restaurants.article_title: Mis mejores restaurantes de Bilbao
